title: When Should You Use AI? The Answer Might Surprise You!
summary:
Have you ever asked a chatbot to help you with homework? A new study says the timing matters a lot! Scientists at the University of Chicago asked 393 people to pretend they were city council members. They had to decide whether to accept a company's plan to clean up polluted water. Some people had 30 minutes to write an essay, while others only had 10 minutes. Some could use an AI chatbot right away, some later, and some not at all.

The results? People who had enough time and used the chatbot later in the process wrote the best essays. They included more different viewpoints. But the group that remembered the most facts was the one that never used the chatbot at all! When time was tight, using AI early helped people get higher scores.

So what's the lesson? If you have time, try to work through a problem on your own first. Then use AI to add more ideas. But if you're really rushed, AI can give you a quick boost. Just be careful—using AI too early might mean you don't think as deeply. Cool, right?
why_it_matters: This story can help you make smarter choices about when to use a chatbot for schoolwork—so you learn more and don’t just copy answers.

keywords.explanation: A chatbot is a computer program that talks to you like a human, often using AI to help answer questions or write things.
Polluted means dirty or unsafe, like water that has harmful chemicals in it.
Viewpoints are different ways of thinking about a problem or idea.
An essay is a short piece of writing that explains what you think about a topic.
A council is a group of people who make decisions for a city or town.
Timing means choosing the right moment to do something.

questions.question: How many people took part in the AI experiment?
What was the job the participants pretended to have?
options: Teacher
Doctor
City council member
Police officer

questions.question: Which group wrote the best essays?
options: People with 10 minutes who used AI early
People with 30 minutes who used AI later in the process
People with 30 minutes who never used AI
People with 10 minutes who used AI later
questions.correct_answer: People with 30 minutes who used AI later in the process

questions.question: Which group remembered the most facts?
options: The group that used AI early
The group that used AI later
The group that never used the chatbot
All groups remembered equally
questions.correct_answer: The group that never used the chatbot

questions.question: What happened when people had less time and used AI early?
options: They got the lowest scores
They got higher scores
They remembered the most facts
They refused to use the chatbot

questions.question: What advice does the article give about using AI?
options: Always use AI before you start a task
Never use AI, it makes you think less
If you have time, work on your own first, then use AI to add more ideas
Use AI only for math, not for writing
questions.correct_answer: If you have time, work on your own first, then use AI to add more ideas

background_read: The University of Chicago (a famous research university in the United States) has done many studies on how people think. This new experiment tested when AI helps or hurts learning.
AI chatbots like ChatGPT have become very popular in schools, but teachers worry that using them too early can stop kids from thinking deeply. Scientists now have a clearer answer about timing.
In real cities, officials often have to make hard choices—like cleaning up polluted water—and they might start using AI tools to help. So this kind of study matters for how decisions get made.
Article_Structure: WHO: 393 people pretending to be city council members at the University of Chicago
WHAT: A study on when using an AI chatbot leads to better essays and learning
WHERE: At the University of Chicago
WHY: To find out if the timing of AI help affects how deeply people think and what they remember

perspectives.description: If you use an AI chatbot after thinking on your own, you can come up with more ideas and write stronger essays. It’s like having a coach who steps in at just the right moment.

perspectives.perspective: Negative – Overuse leads to shallow thinking
perspectives.description: The group that never used a chatbot remembered the most facts. This suggests that relying on AI too much might weaken your memory and deep thinking.
The study shows there is no single answer. When time is short, AI gives a quick boost; when you have time, working alone first produces better thinking. The ideal method depends on the situation.
